metadata.name: Fear — blocking restriction: artifact, black, and non-qualifying blockers
metadata.description: p1 controls Severed Legion (2/2, black Zombie, Fear). p2 controls three potential blockers: Bladetusk Boar (3/2, red, Intimidate — non-artifact, non-black), Solemn Simulacrum (2/2 Artifact Creature — Golem), and Bog Raiders (2/2, black Zombie, Swampwalk). Severed Legion attacks p2. CR 702.36b: a creature with Fear can't be blocked except by artifact creatures and/or black creatures. (1) Bladetusk Boar is red and non-artifact — it cannot block Severed Legion (illegal per 702.36b; documented as turn_based_action). (2) Solemn Simulacrum is an Artifact Creature — it CAN block legally; this case is fully executed via declare_blockers. (3) Bog Raiders is a black creature — it CAN block legally (documented as turn_based_action). Combat damage between Severed Legion and Solemn Simulacrum: each deals 2 damage to the other; both have toughness 2, so both receive lethal damage and are destroyed by SBA 704.5g.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.36b: 'A creature with fear can't be blocked except by artifact creatures and/or black creatures.' Three cases tested: (1) Bladetusk Boar — {3}{R}, Creature — Boar, 3/2, red keyword Intimidate. It is neither an artifact creature nor a black creature, so it cannot block Severed Legion. The engine enforces this in combat.rs: if the attacker has Fear, it checks blocker_is_artifact_creature (Artifact + Creature card types) AND blocker_is_black (colors contains Black). If neither, returns InvalidCommand. This case is modeled as a turn_based_action (documentary) since issuing it as a player_action would produce CommandRejected (test failure). (2) Solemn Simulacrum — {4}, Artifact Creature — Golem, 2/2. It is both CardType::Artifact and CardType::Creature, satisfying the artifact-creature exception. This block is executed as a real declare_blockers player_action. Combat damage: Severed Legion (2 power) deals 2 damage to Solemn Simulacrum (toughness 2 — lethal); Solemn Simulacrum (2 power) deals 2 damage to Severed Legion (toughness 2 — lethal). Both receive lethal damage simultaneously (CR 510.1a). SBA 704.5g destroys both. Solemn Simulacrum's 'dies, draw a card' ETB trigger fires but requires a card in p2's library — no library card is included since the Solemn Simulacrum die trigger is out of scope for this script; the harness will silently skip the draw. Note: we place Solemn Simulacrum in p2's library-less setup and the die trigger may silently not draw, which does not affect zone-change assertions. (3) Bog Raiders — {2}{B}, Creature — Zombie, 2/2, black. It has Color::Black, satisfying the black-creature exception. This case is documented as a turn_based_action; no executable test issued to avoid conflict with the already-declared Solemn Simulacrum block. Severed Legion card definition in definitions.rs: name 'Severed Legion', {1}{B}{B}, Creature — Zombie, 2/2, keyword Fear. Confirmed via MCP lookup_card. Engine implementation confirmed in crates/engine/src/rules/combat.rs.

script.step_note: p1 declares Severed Legion (2/2, Fear) as an attacker targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Severed Legion becomes tapped. Priority resets to p1 after attackers declared.

actions.description: Initial: Severed Legion on p1 battlefield (untapped); Bladetusk Boar, Solemn Simulacrum, Bog Raiders on p2 battlefield; life totals 40/40; stack empty
actions.note: Severed Legion is untapped and free of summoning sickness (placed in initial state by the builder, which sets has_summoning_sickness: false). All three potential blockers are present and untapped on p2's battlefield.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Severed Legion as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1a: Severed Legion is untapped and free of summoning sickness — legal attacker. CR 508.1f: Severed Legion becomes tapped as a side effect of attacking. CR 508.1k: Severed Legion becomes an attacking creature.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Severed Legion is tapped, stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Severed Legion tapped as part of attack declaration. Stack is empty (Severed Legion has Fear keyword only — no attack trigger).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack → advance to DeclareBlockers step. p1 receives priority as active player in DeclareBlockers.

script.step_note: Fear blocking restriction (CR 702.36b). Case (1): Bladetusk Boar cannot block — it is red, non-artifact, non-black. Case (2): Solemn Simulacrum CAN block — it is an artifact creature. Case (3): Bog Raiders CAN block — it is a black creature. Solemn Simulacrum executes the block.

actions.description: ILLEGAL BLOCK — Bladetusk Boar (3/2, red, Intimidate) cannot block Severed Legion (Fear)
actions.note: CR 702.36b: Severed Legion has Fear — it can't be blocked except by artifact creatures and/or black creatures. Bladetusk Boar is a red creature with no artifact types. It is neither an artifact creature (not CardType::Artifact) nor a black creature (Color::Black not in colors). Attempting declare_blockers with Bladetusk Boar would return InvalidCommand from the engine. Documented as turn_based_action (not executed) to avoid harness failure. This is the negative case: non-artifact, non-black creatures are forbidden blockers.

actions.description: LEGAL BLOCK (documented) — Bog Raiders (2/2, black, Swampwalk) CAN block Severed Legion (Fear)
actions.note: CR 702.36b: Bog Raiders is a black creature (mana cost {2}{B}, Color::Black). It satisfies the black-creature exception to Fear. The engine check: blocker_is_black = blocker_chars.colors.contains(Color::Black) — true for Bog Raiders. This block would be legal. Documented as turn_based_action rather than executed to avoid conflict with the Solemn Simulacrum block below (only one declare_blockers per combat).

CR 509.1: p2 declares Solemn Simulacrum as a blocker for Severed Legion. CR 509.1b: Blocker restriction check — Severed Legion has Fear (702.36b). Solemn Simulacrum is an Artifact Creature (CardType::Artifact AND CardType::Creature), satisfying the artifact-creature exception. Block is legal. CR 509.1g: Solemn Simulacrum becomes a blocking creature. CR 509.1h: Severed Legion has one blocker declared — it becomes a blocked creature.

actions.description: After declare_blockers: all creatures still on battlefield, Solemn Simulacrum not tapped (blockers don't tap), stack empty
actions.note: Solemn Simulacrum remains untapped after blocking declaration (blockers do not tap unless a rule/ability requires it, CR 509.1). Bladetusk Boar and Bog Raiders are unaffected. Stack is empty — no blocking triggers from Solemn Simulacrum.

CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack → advance to CombatDamage step. The engine executes combat damage as a turn-based action on entering CombatDamage: Severed Legion (power 2, blocked) assigns 2 damage to Solemn Simulacrum (CR 510.1c); Solemn Simulacrum (power 2) assigns 2 damage to Severed Legion (CR 510.1d). Both are simultaneous (CR 510.1a).

script.step_note: CombatDamage turn-based action: Severed Legion (2 power, blocked) assigns 2 damage to Solemn Simulacrum; Solemn Simulacrum (2 power) assigns 2 damage to Severed Legion. Both have toughness 2 — both receive lethal damage. SBA 704.5g: both destroyed. p2 takes 0 combat damage (blocked attacker assigns damage to blocker, not player).

results.description: Severed Legion has 2 damage marked (equals toughness 2) — lethal damage — destroyed
Solemn Simulacrum has 2 damage marked (equals toughness 2) — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Both creatures have toughness 2 and received exactly 2 damage (equal to toughness) — lethal damage — both destroyed simultaneously (CR 704.3: SBAs checked as batch). Both move to their owners' graveyards. Bladetusk Boar and Bog Raiders are unaffected (were not in combat).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after combat damage resolves and SBAs applied. Stack is empty → advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: Final state: p1 at 40 life, p2 at 40 life (no player damage — Severed Legion was fully blocked), Severed Legion and Solemn Simulacrum destroyed; Bladetusk Boar and Bog Raiders unharmed
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Severed Legion (blocked) assigned all damage to Solemn Simulacrum — p2 took no combat damage (life remains 40). Both combatants destroyed by SBA 704.5g (lethal damage). Bladetusk Boar and Bog Raiders were not involved in combat and remain on p2's battlefield. CR 702.36b tested: the fear restriction correctly excluded Bladetusk Boar from blocking; Solemn Simulacrum (artifact creature exception) and Bog Raiders (black creature exception) were both legal blockers.
